OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO DETERMINE TRUTH OF MATTERS SPECIFIED IN REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION (TRANSACTION ID # 66214996) FILED BY PLAINTIFF BARTEL, DAVID , TRUSTEE OF THE PEGGY BARTEL LIVING TRUST DATED APRIL 17, 2006

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JAN-12-2021 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF DAVID BARTEL'S MOTION FOR ORDER DETERMINING TRUTH OF MATTERS SPECIFIED IN REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION AND FOR SANCTIONS, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER DETERMINING TRUTH OF ADMISSIONS NOT TIMELY RESPONDED TO IS DENIED AS MOOT. VERIFIED RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION WERE SERVED THE FIRST COURT DAY AFTER THE MOTION WAS FILED. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED BASED ON DEFENSE COUNSEL'S STATEMENT UNDER OATH THE SUBJECT REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS SERVED BY EMAIL WAS DIVERTED TO DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL'S SPAM MAIL BOX. WHILE IMPOSITION OF A MONETARY SANCTION AGAINST THE PARTY WHO FAILED TO SERVE A TIMELY RESPONSE IS TECHNICALLY MANDATORY PURSUANT TO CCP SEC. 2033.280(C) THE COURT EXERCISES ITS EQUITABLE DISCRETION TO DENY SAME BASED ON DUE PROCESS RELATING TO FAILURE OF ELECTRONIC SERVICE OF THE DISCOVERY PLEADINGS. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT SBS@SBSLAWSF.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVE STEIN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Determining Truth Of Matters Specified In Requests For Admission And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-12-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF DAVID BARTEL's Motion For Order Determining Truth Of Matters Specified In Requests For Admission And For Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff's Motion for an Order Determining Truth of Admissions not timely responded to is denied as moot. Verified responses to requests for admission were served the first court day after the motion was filed. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is denied based on defense counsel's statement under oath the subject Request for Admissions served by email was diverted to defendant's counsel's spam mail box. While imposition of a monetary sanction against the party who failed to serve a timely response is technically mandatory pursuant to CCP Sec. 2033.280(c) the court exercises its equitable discretion to deny same based on due process relating to failure of electronic service of the discovery pleadings.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at sbs@sbslawsf.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Steve Stein; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-17-2020 Continued To Feb-24-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Determining Truth Of Matters Specified In Requests For Admission And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-29-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DAVID BARTEL's Motion For Order Determining Truth Of Matters Specified In Requests For Admission And For Sanctions:

NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED TO PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER DETERMINING THE TRUTH OF MATTERS SPECIEIE IN REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION WHICH HAVE REMAINED UNANSWERED FOR MORE THAN 40 DAYS. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT WILL ISSUE THE FOLLOWING ORDER:
1. WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER DEFENDANT 2046 FILBERT LLC WILL SERVE ON PLAINTIFF DAVE BARTEL, TRUSTEE FULL AND COMPLETE CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSE TO THE FIVE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION.
2. IF DEFENDANT FAILS TO COMPLY WITH PARAGRAPH (1) ABOVE, THE TRUTH OF THE MATTERS SPECIFIED IN THE FIVE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION WILL BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND DEFENDANT WILL BE PRECLUDED FROM CHALLENGING THE TRUTH OF THESE ADMISSIONS IN THIS LITIGATION.
3. THE COURT WILL ASSESS MONETARY DAMAGES IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,500.00 PAYMENT OF WHICH SHALL BE MADE BY DEFENDANT AND ITS ATTORNEYS WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER TO PLAINTIFF AND HIS ATTORNEYS.

COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT IT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT AND SUBMIT TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING VIA email to rennepar85@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

***per email clarification between counsel and JPT on 12/30/2020, amended notice of this motion is re-set for Jan. 12, 2021. Therefore this ruling is vacated.***
